knowledge_text: Product names are in Products; count distinct OrderNumber and filter with HAVING.
column_value_notes: `Sales Orders`.`Unit Price`: TEXT numeric values may include commas, e.g. "1,005.00"; for numeric aggregation use CAST(REPLACE(T1.`Unit Price`, ',', '') AS REAL). 7991/7991 non-NULL, 664 distinct.
`Sales Orders`.`Unit Cost`: TEXT numeric values may include commas, e.g. "1,001.18"; for numeric aggregation use CAST(REPLACE(T1.`Unit Cost`, ',', '') AS REAL). 7991/7991 non-NULL, 5252 distinct.

`Sales Orders`.`Order Quantity`: exact identifier contains spaces or punctuation; use backticks as T1.`Order Quantity`, not OrderQuantity. Declared INTEGER; 7991/7991 non-NULL, 8 distinct; sample values: 8, 1, 3.

`Sales Orders`.`Sales Channel`: exact identifier contains spaces or punctuation; use backticks as T1.`Sales Channel`, not SalesChannel. Declared TEXT; 7991/7991 non-NULL, 4 distinct; sample values: In-Store, Online, Distributor.

`Customers`.`Customer Names`: exact identifier contains spaces or punctuation; use backticks as T4.`Customer Names`, not CustomerNames. Declared TEXT; 50/50 non-NULL, 50 distinct; sample values: 21st Ltd, 3LAB, Ltd, Amylin Group.

`Products`.`Product Name`: exact identifier contains spaces or punctuation; use backticks as T5.`Product Name`, not ProductName. Declared TEXT; 47/47 non-NULL, 47 distinct; sample values: Accessories, Audio, Bakeware.
gold_sql: SELECT T4.`Product Name` FROM `Sales Orders` AS T1 INNER JOIN `Store Locations` AS T2 ON T1._StoreID = T2.StoreID INNER JOIN Regions AS T3 ON T2.StateCode = T3.StateCode INNER JOIN Products AS T4 ON T1._ProductID = T4.ProductID WHERE T3.Region = 'West' GROUP BY T4.`Product Name` HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.OrderNumber) > 2
